Shared-stg-DB integration concurrency flake (qualifies the parallelize-everything directive). PR #1184 (WP-3) main went RED on identity-reseed.integration.test.ts (membership_person_id FK violation in the reseed-converge setup) — NOT PR-caused (WP-3 diff = archive-cleanup.ts only; main PASSED on #1185 same base). Root cause: api:test:integration runs against the SHARED stg DB; with many parallel PRs the identity-reseed converge test (full legacy→8-table reseed touching person/membership) gets interfered with → intermittent FK violations. Handling: re-ran #1184's failed main job (flaky-retry); if integration flakes recur across the fleet I will THROTTLE concurrency on integration-touching PRs (serialize their CI) rather than blanket-parallelize. Unit-only / other-domain PRs unaffected.

Cutover-flip-mid-grace-period hazard (surfaced by WP-4 review, Codex P1) — flag for the cutover-plan owner. Account deletion has a 7-day grace period; the v2 wiring re-reads IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED at execution time rather than pinning the mode at schedule time, so a flag flip (the cutover flip OR a rollback) mid-grace-period misroutes the deletion → a GDPR/COPPA erasure can be silently skipped. Being fixed in WP-4 (pin the identity mode at schedule, use it for every step + regression test). CROSS-CUTTING for the cutover plan / WP-FLAG timing: the flip must account for in-flight grace-period operations scheduled in the OLD mode — either drain/quiesce them before the flip, or guarantee mode-pinning on every multi-day/scheduled flow that spans the flip. Deletion is the clearest case; candidate siblings to audit before WP-FLAG: scheduled exports, grace-period/trial billing transitions, any Inngest flow that persists a 'pending' decision across the flip window. Not blocking the lane; raising so the cutover runbook covers in-flight-op drain/pinning.

SHARED-CHECKOUT COMMIT INCIDENT + FLEET HAZARD (operator decision). WP-8 executor's /commit skill mis-targeted the shared MAIN checkout (not its .worktrees/WI-777) and pushed 6def49340 to origin/main (now HEAD), sweeping cross-session uncommitted WIP: reviewer-watcher session files (review-watcher-v3.ts, review-loop-*.md) + this lane's inbox/outbox.jsonl channel state. LOW impact (all _wip tooling/docs + channel files, NO production code; won't conflict with in-flight PRs). NOT reverting (reverting is the destructive act; content is legitimate, landed via wrong actor) — recommend LEAVE + notify reviewer-watcher/orchestrator sessions. REAL ISSUE = fleet hazard: with 5+ parallel executors on the shared checkout, a /commit resolving to the main working tree could next sweep PRODUCTION code onto main. Mitigation options: (a) executors assert CWD = worktree / commit via git -C <worktree> before /commit; (b) fully-isolated clones per executor; (c) accept + monitor. WP-8 verification PR #1187 itself is unaffected (correctly on WI-777 branch). DECISIONS NEEDED: (1) leave-vs-handle 6def49340; (2) fleet commit-isolation mitigation.

Cutover-completeness gap surfaced by WP-9 (WI-778) review (Codex P1). The quota satellite tables quotaPools + profileQuotaUsage still FK the LEGACY subscriptions table (packages/database/src/schema/billing.ts:100,132); nothing has rehomed them to the v2 subscription table. Two implications: (1) WP-9 in-lane fix = seed-side dual-write of a legacy subscriptions row for quota scenarios (executor handling now; conservative, no scope creep). (2) CROSS-CUTTING for the terminal data half (WI-586): the legacy subscriptions DROP CANNOT proceed until quotaPools/profileQuotaUsage are rehomed to v2 subscription (FK-target table cannot be dropped while referenced) — this is a hard ordering precondition on WI-586, analogous to the accounts-before-cutover ordering. Likely a small migration (repoint the 2 FKs) that belongs to WI-586 scope or a dedicated billing-schema WP, NOT WP-9 and NOT WP-7 (read-path only). Flagging so WI-586 AC captures the rehome-before-drop step. Not blocking the WAVE.

RECURRENCE of the shared-checkout commit-isolation hazard + evidence the ic-orch-008 brief-level mitigation is INSUFFICIENT. WI-780 executor: the repo /commit skill ran AS A FORK and committed af085575c ("chore(_wip): session artifacts...") against the MAIN shared checkout instead of .worktrees/WI-780, sweeping other sessions uncommitted _wip/.cosmo/identity-cutover artifacts (incl this lane channel-state). KEY DIFF from 6def49340: af085575c is LOCAL-ONLY, NOT pushed — origin/main clean, no remote impact. Executor recovered its real deliverable correctly+isolated in the worktree (PR #1192 @6d35e12d1) and deliberately did NOT unwind af085575c (reverting another session commit on shared main is itself the hazardous act). ROOT CAUSE (new, important): ic-orch-008 mitigation = the EXECUTOR asserts git -C worktree toplevel before /commit, but the repo /commit skill runs as a FORK that does NOT inherit the executor worktree CWD — the executor assertion never governs the fork actual git invocation. Brief-level fix cannot close this; durable fix must harden the /commit skill/flow itself (the estate-level follow-up ic-orch-008 deferred as not-now — this recurrence argues it IS now). RECOMMEND: (1) LEAVE af085575c local-unpushed (do not unwind; legitimate misplaced WIP; local-only=no remote impact); operator decides keep/re-scope/drop BEFORE any git push of main. (2) No session should push origin main until adjudicated (PR merges are server-side squashes, unaffected). (3) Pull the /commit-fork worktree-binding fix forward. DECISIONS NEEDED: disposition of af085575c + whether to harden the /commit skill now.

Recurring test-isolation flake CONFIRMED + prg06ic-007 mechanism CORRECTED. identity-reseed.integration.test.ts (all 9 "0109 legacy→8-table" tests) failed in #1190 (WP-7) main with error: insert or update on table membership violates foreign key constraint membership_person_id_person_id_fk in the reseed SETUP — identical signature to prg06ic-007 (WP-3 #1184). CORRECTED MECHANISM: not shared-stg-DB. CI api:test:integration runs jest maxWorkers=2 against ONE LOCAL postgres (localhost:5432/tests); the reseed suite does a full-DB legacy→8-table convergence asserting exact GLOBAL counts, so a concurrent suite on the other worker races/contaminates the identity tables → reseed inserts a membership for a person row another worker removed → FK violation → all 9 fail at setup. NOT PR-caused (WP-7 = metering/refund; untouched by reseed). Re-ran the failed job (flaky-retry per prg06ic-007). Now 2+ fleet hits; re-runs are a band-aid that taxes every integration-touching PR. RECOMMEND a tracked fix: isolate the reseed suite from concurrent suites — dedicated DB/schema per worker, OR pin it to a serial (--runInBand / single-worker) jest project, OR make its setup atomically truncate+rebuild under an advisory lock. I can instantiate a WS-18 (or test-infra) Item. Not blocking (re-run pending); flagging the systemic cost.

MAIN-RED INCIDENT + I OWN A WRONG ADJUDICATION. origin/main (b2612998e = WP-9/WI-778 squash) push run 27556706563 FAILED on test-seed.integration.test.ts with FK violations subjects_profile_id_profiles_id_fk (subjects->profiles) + quota_pools_subscription_id_subscriptions_id_fk (quota_pools->subscriptions) — the EXACT Codex P1 I adjudicated invalid on WP-9. My error: I reasoned from the STAGING cutover state (legacy dropped + FKs re-pointed by the STAGING-ONLY manual m-repoint.sql), but the LOCAL CI integration DB (localhost:5432/tests) runs the COMMITTED migrations fresh — legacy profiles/subscriptions tables PRESENT, their FKs from subjects/quota_pools INTACT, and m-repoint is NOT a committed migration so it never runs there. WP-9 v2-only test-seed writes person/subscription[v2] but NOT the legacy profiles/subscriptions parents those FKs require -> insert fails. WP-9 #1189 BRANCH main passed test-seed.integration (likely contamination-luck — another suite transiently left legacy rows), so the regression slipped the gate; b2612998e main fails it. ROOT CAUSE (broader): the staging cutover (m-repoint + M-DROP) was MANUAL/ahead-of-committed-migrations, so the committed-migration CI/PROD DBs still have legacy tables + legacy FKs that the v2 code/seed cannot satisfy. IMPACT: main is RED/fragile on test-seed.integration -> blocks EVERY api PR main (WP-5 #1191 included). FIX OPTIONS: (A) FAST — WP-9 fast-follow: make the v2 test-seed CONDITIONALLY write the legacy profiles/subscriptions parent rows WHEN those tables exist (detect table/FK target; safe on post-DROP staging where it skips) -> deterministically green on CI, safe on staging, localized to the seed. (B) PROPER — commit m-repoint as a real migration so CI/PROD re-point subjects/quota_pools FKs to v2 (WI-586 cutover-productionization; bigger, and pairs with the M-DROP migration). RECOMMEND A now to unblock main, with B as the WI-586 close work. Re-running the b2612998e main to confirm deterministic-vs-contamination (signal in ~10m). Holding WP-5 merge (blocked on main). STEER NEEDED: approve fix (A) seed-workaround dispatch now, or prefer (B)? Not merging anything until main is green.

CI currently runs integration flag-OFF; the close gate must add a flag-ON committed-migration integration run. I will encode this in WI-586 close criteria (replacing the staging-sweep framing).
